title: Prompt engineering to inform large language model in automated building energy modeling
inputData: 文本（自然语言建筑描述+IDF文件示例）
contribution: 首次系统研究prompt工程在LLM自动化建筑能耗建模中的应用，设计了6种prompt策略（zero-shot、one-shot、few-shot、task explanation、task division）并测试18种开源轻量级LLM，共648个案例，发现one-shot学习是最高效的ABEM策略，且紧凑型LLM（如Llama3、Mistral-v0.3）在适当上下文窗口下可有效完成真实建筑建模任务
abstract: Application of large language models (LLMs) to facilitate auto-building energy modeling (ABEM) is complex and resource-intensive. This paper presents practical guidelines for ABEM using prompt engineering with LLMs. Unlike training LLMs for ABEM with fine-tuning (involving model weight adjustment), prompt engineering is to provide specific prompts along with modeling descriptions and demonstrations, informing LLMs to automatically generate building energy models (BEMs) through natural language expression, and enabling users to perform ABEM without specialized building knowledge or software proficiency. In this study, the capabilities of prompt engineering for ABEM using LLMs are investigated. To achieve this, six types of prompts are designed, encompassing two exploratory tasks and one real-world task with a total of 648 case studies. The results from the case studies suggest that prompt engineering is feasible to automatically obtain the desired BEMs using one-shot learning (one demonstration), few-shot learning (few-demonstration), and chain-of-thought strategies (i.e., task explanation and division). Finally, the modeling comparison between the tested lightweighted LLMs with GPT-4o suggests that compact LLMs with an appropriate context window are suitable to be deployed for various building applications.
methodology: The study designs six prompt engineering strategies (zero-shot, one-shot, two-shot, three-shot, task explanation, and task division) and tests them on 18 instruction-tuned lightweight open-source LLMs (6B-14B parameters) across three ABEM tasks of increasing complexity: simple geometry modeling (Task 1), complex building modeling with internal loads (Task 2), and real-world building modeling of an NREL iUnit test facility (Task 3). A total of 648 test instances are evaluated using qualitative indicators (Perfect/Moderate/Bad) based on whether the generated IDF files can be successfully executed by EnergyPlus. An additional comparison with GPT-4o is conducted to benchmark open-source compact models against large-scale closed-source models.
results: Zero-shot learning failed completely for all LLMs. One-shot learning achieved approximately 25% 'Perfect' results, while two-shot improved to 31.5% 'Perfect' but with increased memory issues (16.7% OOM/TL). Llama-3-8B-Instruct is the best-performing model, achieving 'Perfect' results in real-world Task 3 with one-shot learning. Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 achieved 'Perfect' in Task 3 with three-shot learning. Larger model sizes and longer context windows do not necessarily lead to better performance. GPT-4o underperformed compared to lightweight open-source LLMs, frequently generating excessive irrelevant content. One-shot is identified as the most efficient strategy for ABEM, requiring only a single demonstration.
markdownFile: Jiang, G. etal - 2025 - Prompt engineering to inform large language model in automated building energy modeling
inputDataEn: Text (natural language building descriptions + IDF file examples)
pdfFile: Jiang, G. etal - 2025 - Prompt engineering to inform large language model in automated building energy modeling
